timeline.label: Q1 2026 10-Q: net income $319M, EPS $2.34; cash $1.09B; long-term debt reduced to $1.81B
FY2025 10-K: net sales $5.14B, operating loss $367M, operating cash flow $1.28B; lithium trough pricing
FY2024 10-K: net sales $5.38B, operating loss $1.78B; deeper lithium downturn year
Cost and capital discipline pivot as lithium prices fell from 2022 peak; restructuring charges
FY2022 peak cycle baseline: net sales $9.62B, net income $1.57B; heavy conversion capex ramp
Equity offering documented in 424B5 shelf takedown during lithium up-cycle expansion
Prior equity offering during lithium demand surge (424B5)
Energy storage segment growth strategy accelerated pre-2022 lithium price spike

industry.structure: Lithium conversion is concentrated among Albemarle, SQM, Tianqi/Livent successors, and Chinese integrators; bromine and specialty catalysts provide oligopoly cash in adjacent segments.
industry.share_shift: Chinese supply growth and EV demand volatility shifted pricing power from producers to battery makers during 2023–2025; incumbents with integrated resources retain cost advantages.
industry.trend: Lithium spot prices collapsed from 2022 peaks; producers cut capex and delay greenfield while EV adoption continues on a slower ramp; bromine and specialties partially offset energy storage losses.
